    Module: cop_contrisk

    module-cop-contrisk.Rmd

    Purpose

    cop_contrisk estimates controlled risk curves that describe how vaccine efficacy varies as a function of a continuous immune marker. For each marker it produces both Cox-model-based and nonparametric estimates of the covariate-adjusted cumulative incidence in the investigational and comparator vaccine arms, plotted against the weighted marker density of per-protocol participants.

    Unlike most modules in correlates_reporting2, cop_contrisk is organised as a set of self-contained per-trial Rmd notebooks, each located in a trial-specific subdirectory rather than a shared code/ directory.

    Source module: correlates_reporting2/cop_contrisk/
    Key reports:

    Trial subdirectory Rmd
    vaxart_mock/ controlled_risk_vaxart_mock.Rmd

    Key packages

    The analysis Rmd depends on the vaccine package for controlled-risk estimation (est_ce(), est_overall(), plot_ce()) and on dplyr, ggplot2, and patchwork for data manipulation and plotting.

    Reproduce the report

    1. Configure the session 

    library(utilrep2)

report_root <- "/path/to/correlates_reporting2"
setup_session(
  trial       = "nextgen_mock",
  platform    = "linux",
  data_path   = file.path(report_root, "data_clean"),
  report_root = report_root
)

    2. Run the module 

    run_cop_contrisk(trial = "nextgen_mock")

    This renders cop_contrisk/vaxart_mock/controlled_risk_vaxart_mock.Rmd and writes a date-stamped PDF to the same subdirectory, e.g.:

    cop_contrisk/vaxart_mock/controlled_risk_vaxart_mock_20260322.pdf

    To write the PDF to a different location, pass output_dir:

    run_cop_contrisk(
  trial      = "nextgen_mock",
  output_dir = "/results/contrisk/2026-03-22"
)

    What the Rmd does

    The report reads the analysis-ready dataset via config::get() pointing at config.yml in the project root, then runs the following sequence for each of three marker contrasts:

    Marker Contrast
    Bpseudoneutid50_sera_XBB.1.5 Baseline (D01) nAb-ID50
    Day31pseudoneutid50_sera_XBB.1.5 Post-vaccination (D31) nAb-ID50
    Delta31overBpseudoneutid50_sera_XBB.1.5 Fold-change D31/D01 nAb-ID50

    For each marker and each method (Cox, Nonparametric):

    1. Subset to Phase-1 per-protocol participants (ph1.D31_7 == 1).
    2. Load data via vaccine::load_data(), setting Phase-2 indicator and sampling weights for the sera subcohort.
    3. Estimate overall incidence in each arm via vaccine::est_overall().
    4. Estimate controlled risk via vaccine::est_ce().
    5. Truncate to the middle 95% of the weighted marker distribution.
    6. Plot using vaccine::plot_ce() with overlaid arm-specific incidence lines and a kernel density ribbon.

    The helper function CoR_D1() (defined inside the Rmd) encapsulates steps 2–6 and saves an individual PDF to the Plot/ subdirectory.

    Output

    Location Contents
    cop_contrisk/vaxart_mock/Plot/*.pdf Per-marker controlled-risk plots
    cop_contrisk/vaxart_mock/controlled_risk_vaxart_mock_<date>.pdf Full assembled report

    Supported trials

    Trial label Subdirectory Rmd
    nextgen_mock vaxart_mock/ controlled_risk_vaxart_mock.Rmd

    Developed by Y H.

    Site built with pkgdown 2.2.0.
